recipes.title: Butter Chicken
recipes.description: Creamy tomato-based curry with tender tandoori-style chicken pieces simmered in a rich, buttery sauce.
steps: Marinate chicken in yogurt, half the ginger garlic paste, chili powder, and salt for at least 1 hour.
Sear marinated chicken in a hot pan or under the grill until lightly charred on the edges.
In a separate pan, melt butter and saute remaining ginger garlic paste for one minute.
Add tomato puree and simmer for 10 minutes until oil separates and the sauce deepens in color.
Add the grilled chicken pieces and cook for 5 minutes to absorb the sauce.
Stir in cream, garam masala, and crushed kasuri methi, then simmer 3 more minutes.
Adjust salt and finish with a knob of butter before serving with naan or basmati rice.

recipes.title: Chicken Biryani
recipes.description: Fragrant layered rice dish with marinated chicken, saffron, and aromatic whole spices, slow-cooked dum style.
steps: Marinate chicken with yogurt, ginger garlic paste, biryani masala, half the fried onions, and salt for 2 hours.
Soak basmati rice for 30 minutes, then parboil with whole spices and salt until 70 percent cooked.
Bloom saffron in warm milk and set aside.
Cook the marinated chicken in a heavy pot over medium heat until partially tender.
Layer parboiled rice over the chicken, top with mint, coriander, fried onions, ghee, and saffron milk.
Seal the pot with a tight lid or dough and cook on the lowest heat (dum) for 25 minutes.
Rest 10 minutes, then gently fluff to mix layers and serve with raita.

recipes.title: Chana Masala
recipes.description: Spiced chickpea curry with tomatoes, onions, and a punchy blend of dry-roasted Punjabi spices.
steps: Soak chickpeas overnight, then pressure cook with salt and a tea bag until soft.
Heat oil and crackle cumin seeds, then add finely chopped onions and saute until deeply golden.
Add ginger garlic paste and cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in pureed tomatoes and chana masala, cooking until oil separates from the masala.
Add cooked chickpeas with some cooking liquid and simmer 15 minutes to thicken.
Adjust salt and finish with a squeeze of lemon and chopped coriander.

recipes.title: Rogan Josh
recipes.description: Aromatic Kashmiri lamb curry coloured with kashmiri chillies and ratan jot, finished with fennel and ginger.
steps: Heat ghee and bloom whole cloves, cardamoms, and asafoetida.
Brown the lamb pieces in batches over high heat, then return all to the pot.
Mix kashmiri chili with a little water to a paste and add to the pot, cooking 2 minutes.
Whisk yogurt with fennel and ginger powder, then add gradually while stirring to prevent splitting.
Pour in enough hot water to almost cover the lamb and bring to a simmer.
Cover and cook on low for 90 minutes until lamb is fork tender and the sauce is glossy red.
Adjust salt and serve with steamed rice.

recipes.title: Dal Makhani
recipes.description: Slow-cooked black lentils and kidney beans simmered with butter and cream for a deeply silky finish.
steps: Soak urad dal and kidney beans overnight in plenty of water.
Pressure cook the soaked legumes with salt for 30 minutes until completely soft.
In a heavy pot, melt half the butter, add ginger garlic paste and saute briefly.
Add tomato puree and chili powder, cooking until oil separates.
Add the cooked dal with its liquid and simmer on the lowest heat for 1 hour, mashing occasionally.
Stir in cream, garam masala, kasuri methi, and the remaining butter.
Simmer 10 more minutes and finish with a swirl of cream.

recipes.title: Aloo Gobi
recipes.description: Dry-style Punjabi stir-fry of potatoes and cauliflower spiced with turmeric, cumin, and ginger.
steps: Heat oil in a wide pan and crackle cumin seeds.
Add chopped onion and saute until light golden, then add grated ginger.
Stir in chopped tomato, turmeric, coriander, and salt and cook until pulpy.
Add diced potatoes and cauliflower florets and toss to coat with the masala.
Cover and cook on low heat for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally so vegetables steam through.
Uncover and stir-fry 5 minutes to dry out and crisp the edges.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve with roti.

recipes.title: Samosa
recipes.description: Crisp golden pastry triangles stuffed with spiced potatoes, peas, and roasted cumin.
steps: Rub ghee into flour with salt, then add a little water at a time to form a stiff dough; rest 30 minutes.
Boil and roughly mash potatoes, then mix with peas, chopped chili, cumin, garam masala, and amchur.
Divide dough into balls, roll each into an oval, and cut in half to create cones.
Fill each cone with the potato mixture and seal the edges with water.
Heat oil to 140 C and fry samosas on low for 12 minutes until they begin to blister.
Increase oil temperature to 180 C and fry again until deep golden brown.
Drain on paper and serve with tamarind and mint chutneys.

recipes.title: Masala Dosa
recipes.description: Crisp fermented rice and lentil crepe filled with spiced potato masala, served with chutney and sambar.
steps: Soak rice, urad dal, and fenugreek separately for 6 hours.
Grind to a smooth batter, combine, salt lightly, and ferment overnight in a warm place.
Boil potatoes and roughly mash. Temper mustard seeds and curry leaves in oil, add onions and turmeric.
Mix mashed potato into the tempered onions to make the masala filling.
Heat a flat griddle, ladle batter and spread thin in a circle.
Drizzle ghee, place a portion of potato masala in the center, fold and crisp until golden.
Serve immediately with coconut chutney and sambar.

recipes.title: Idli
recipes.description: Soft steamed cakes of fermented rice and urad dal, a beloved South Indian breakfast staple.
steps: Soak rice and fenugreek seeds together; soak urad dal separately, both for 6 hours.
Grind urad dal to a fluffy paste, then grind rice to a slightly grainy paste.
Combine, add salt, and ferment in a warm spot for 8 to 12 hours until doubled.
Grease idli plates and ladle batter into the molds.
Steam for 12 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean.
Cool slightly, unmold, and serve with sambar and coconut chutney.

recipes.title: Vada Pav
recipes.description: Mumbai street snack of a spiced potato fritter sandwiched in a soft pav with green and garlic chutneys.
steps: Boil and mash potatoes. Temper mustard seeds and curry leaves in oil with ginger garlic paste.
Add chopped chilies and turmeric, then mix in mashed potato and salt.
Cool the mixture and shape into balls.
Whisk chickpea flour with water, salt, and a pinch of turmeric to a thick coating batter.
Dip potato balls in batter and deep fry at 180 C until crisp and golden.
Slice pav, smear with green chutney and garlic chutney, and sandwich a hot vada inside.
Serve immediately with fried chilies.

recipes.title: Pav Bhaji
recipes.description: Spiced mashed vegetable curry served with toasted buttered pav buns, a Mumbai street icon.
steps: Pressure cook potatoes, cauliflower, and peas with salt until soft, then mash.
On a tawa, melt butter and saute chopped onions until translucent, then add capsicum.
Add chopped tomatoes and pav bhaji masala, cooking until pulpy and oil separates.
Add the mashed vegetables with a little water and cook 10 minutes, mashing further on the tawa.
Slit pav buns, butter both sides, and toast on a hot tawa until crisp and golden.
Top bhaji with chopped onion, coriander, lemon, and a knob of butter.
Serve hot with the toasted pav.

recipes.title: Palak Paneer
recipes.description: Fresh paneer cubes simmered in a smooth, vibrant spinach gravy with garlic, ginger, and warm spices.
steps: Blanch spinach and chilies in boiling water for 2 minutes, then plunge into ice water.
Blend spinach and chilies into a smooth puree.
Heat ghee and crackle cumin seeds, then add chopped onions and cook until soft.
Add ginger garlic paste and chopped tomato; cook until tomato breaks down.
Pour in spinach puree with a splash of water and simmer 8 minutes.
Add cubed paneer, garam masala, salt, and cream; simmer 5 more minutes.
Finish with a drizzle of cream and serve with naan or jeera rice.

recipes.title: Tandoori Chicken
recipes.description: Yogurt and spice marinated chicken charred in a tandoor or oven for smoky, juicy results.
steps: Make deep slashes in the chicken legs and rub with lemon juice, salt, and half the chili powder.
Whisk yogurt with ginger garlic paste, garam masala, remaining chili, and mustard oil.
Coat chicken thoroughly with the marinade and refrigerate at least 6 hours.
Preheat oven to 220 C with a wire rack over a tray.
Roast chicken for 25 minutes, basting once with melted butter halfway through.
Switch to grill mode for the final 5 minutes for char.
Rest 5 minutes and serve with onion rings, mint chutney, and lemon wedges.

recipes.title: Chicken Tikka Masala
recipes.description: Grilled chicken tikka simmered in a creamy tomato curry seasoned with fenugreek and garam masala.
steps: Marinate diced chicken in yogurt and 2 tbsp tikka paste for 1 hour.
Skewer the chicken and grill or pan sear until charred and just cooked through.
Heat ghee and saute finely chopped onion until golden, then add ginger garlic paste.
Add remaining tikka paste and tomato puree; simmer until the oil separates.
Stir in cream and a splash of water and bring to a gentle simmer.
Add the grilled chicken and cook 5 minutes to absorb the gravy.
Crush kasuri methi over the top and serve with naan.

recipes.title: Pakora
recipes.description: Crisp chickpea flour fritters loaded with onions, potatoes, and spices, eaten with chutney.
steps: Slice onions thinly and grate the potato; chop chilies and spinach.
Combine chickpea flour, ajwain, chili powder, and salt in a bowl.
Add the vegetables and mix without water; the moisture from onions binds the batter.
Add a few tablespoons of water only if needed to form thick clumps.
Heat oil to 170 C and drop in spoonfuls of the mixture.
Fry until deep golden and crisp, about 4 minutes, turning once.
Drain and serve hot with mint chutney and masala chai.

recipes.title: Chole Bhature
recipes.description: Punjabi feast of spicy chickpea curry served with deep-fried, fluffy leavened bread.
steps: Soak chickpeas overnight, then pressure cook with a tea bag and salt until soft.
Make bhatura dough with flour, yogurt, salt, a pinch of baking soda, and water; rest 2 hours.
For chole, saute onion in oil, add ginger garlic paste, then tomatoes and chana masala.
Add cooked chickpeas with liquid and simmer until thick and dark.
Roll bhatura dough into ovals and deep fry in 190 C oil until puffed and golden.
Drain bhatura on paper towels.
Serve hot chole with bhature, sliced onions, and pickle.

recipes.title: Pani Puri
recipes.description: Crisp hollow puris filled with spicy tamarind water, potato, and chickpeas — India's iconic chaat.
steps: Mash potatoes lightly and mix with chickpeas, chaat masala, and salt.
Blend mint, coriander, chili, tamarind, and black salt with cold water for the pani.
Strain the pani and chill until ready to serve.
Tap a small hole in the top of each puri shell.
Fill each puri with a spoon of potato mixture.
Dip filled puri into the chilled pani and eat immediately, one bite at a time.

recipes.title: Chicken Korma
recipes.description: Mughal-style mild curry with chicken simmered in a creamy cashew, yogurt, and aromatic spice gravy.
steps: Soak cashews in warm water and grind to a smooth paste with the fried onions.
Heat ghee and bloom cardamom and cinnamon, then add ginger garlic paste.
Add chicken and brown lightly on all sides.
Whisk yogurt with the cashew onion paste and stir into the pan over low heat.
Cover and simmer 25 minutes until chicken is tender and gravy is glossy.
Sprinkle garam masala and rest 5 minutes off the heat.
Serve with sheermal or pulao.

recipes.title: Rasgulla
recipes.description: Soft, spongy chhena dumplings poached in light cardamom-scented sugar syrup, a Bengali classic.
steps: Boil milk, then turn off heat and stir in lemon juice until it curdles cleanly.
Strain through muslin and rinse with cold water to remove any sourness.
Hang the chhena for 30 minutes to drain, then knead until smooth and pliable.
Roll into 12 small smooth balls without cracks.
Boil sugar with water and cardamom in a wide pan until the syrup is thin.
Drop chhena balls into the simmering syrup, cover, and cook 18 minutes; they will double in size.
Cool in the syrup for several hours before serving chilled.

recipes.title: Gulab Jamun
recipes.description: Golden milk-solid dumplings deep fried and soaked in rose and cardamom-scented sugar syrup.
steps: Combine milk powder, flour, baking soda, and ghee, then add milk gradually to form a soft dough.
Rest dough 10 minutes, then divide and roll into smooth crack-free balls.
Make syrup by simmering sugar, water, cardamom, and rose water for 5 minutes.
Heat oil to 130 C — low temperature is critical for even cooking.
Fry the balls slowly, swirling oil over them, until evenly deep brown.
Drain briefly and immediately drop into the warm syrup.
Soak at least 1 hour before serving warm or at room temperature.

recipes.title: Jalebi
recipes.description: Crispy fermented batter swirls deep fried until crunchy, then soaked in saffron sugar syrup.
steps: Whisk flour, yogurt, and water into a smooth thick batter and ferment overnight.
Make syrup by simmering sugar, water, saffron, cardamom, and lemon juice to one-string consistency.
Pour batter into a piping bottle or cone with a fine tip.
Heat ghee in a wide shallow pan to 180 C.
Pipe batter directly into the ghee in small spiral shapes.
Fry until crisp and golden, then transfer immediately into the warm syrup for 30 seconds.
Lift out, drain, and serve hot.

recipes.title: Malai Kofta
recipes.description: Soft paneer and potato dumplings simmered in a rich, mildly sweet creamy tomato cashew gravy.
steps: Grate paneer and boiled potato together, mix with cornflour and salt to form a smooth dough.
Shape into small balls and deep fry in oil at 170 C until golden; set aside.
Boil onion, tomato, and cashews together, then blend to a smooth puree.
Cook the puree with ginger garlic paste in a little oil until thick and oil separates.
Add cream, water, salt, and kasuri methi; simmer 5 minutes.
Add koftas just before serving so they remain soft.
Garnish with cream and serve with naan.

recipes.title: Naan
recipes.description: Soft, leavened tandoor-baked flatbread with yogurt and a charred surface, the classic Indian bread.
steps: Combine flour, salt, sugar, yeast, yogurt, and milk; knead to a soft dough.
Cover and let rise 1 hour until doubled.
Divide into 8 balls and roll each into a teardrop shape.
Heat a cast iron tawa or skillet on high until smoking hot.
Brush one side of the naan with water and stick to the hot pan.
Flip the pan upside down over a flame to char the top, or finish under a hot grill.
Brush with ghee, sprinkle nigella seeds, and serve hot.

recipes.title: Kheer
recipes.description: Slow-simmered rice pudding with milk, sugar, cardamom, saffron, and toasted nuts.
steps: Wash and soak rice for 20 minutes, then drain.
Toast rice briefly in ghee in a heavy pot.
Add milk and bring to a boil, then reduce to a gentle simmer.
Cook 40 minutes, stirring frequently, until rice is soft and milk has thickened.
Add sugar, cardamom, and saffron and simmer 5 more minutes.
Stir in slivered almonds and chopped pistachios.
Serve warm or chilled, garnished with extra nuts.

recipes.title: Aloo Paratha
recipes.description: Whole wheat flatbread stuffed with spiced potato filling, pan-cooked with ghee until golden and flaky.
steps: Knead flour with water and salt into a soft dough; rest 20 minutes.
Boil and mash potatoes, then mix with chili, ginger, coriander, amchur, garam masala, and salt.
Take a ball of dough, flatten it, and place a generous portion of filling in the center.
Pinch dough closed around the filling and roll out gently into a round paratha.
Cook on a hot tawa, drizzling ghee on both sides, until golden brown spots appear.
Press lightly with a spatula so the paratha cooks evenly.
Serve hot with yogurt, butter, and pickle.
